meta.title: The Tarnished's Road
meta.subtitle: One route, one build, no wasted steps. Follow the golden thread from the Chapel of Anticipation to the Elden Throne — every stop, every item, every level, in order.
meta.pageTitle: The Tarnished's Road — An Optimal Elden Ring Walkthrough
meta.footerText: An original walkthrough written for elden-ring newcomers and efficiency runners alike.

blocks.html: The single most forgiving, efficient way through Elden Ring: a tanky knight wielding Bloodhound's Fang, backed by the Mimic Tear. High damage, built-in bleed, a dodge baked into your weapon skill, and a clone of yourself tanking every boss.

Bloodhound's Fang is a curved greatsword you can grab within the first hour. It deals huge damage, causes blood loss (a chunk of the enemy's health bar when the meter fills), and its weapon skill — Bloodhound's Finesse — is an attack that doubles as a backflip dodge. It upgrades with Somber Smithing Stones, which means only ten stones total to max it, no farming dozens of regular stones.
The Mimic Tear spirit ash summons a copy of you, with your weapon and gear. From the mid-game onward, every boss fight becomes two-on-one in your favor. Until then, the Lone Wolf Ashes carry you.

items.html: Class: Vagabond. Best starting stats for this build — high Vigor, solid Strength and Dexterity, good armor.
Keepsake: Golden Seed. A free flask charge from minute one. (Nothing else comes close.)

blocks.text: Stat priority — level in this exact order

rows: Survive Limgrave. Vigor is the single best stat in the game — never skimp on it.
The exact requirements to wield Bloodhound's Fang.
Your mid-game health pool. Do this before Leyndell.
More stamina for swings and dodges; enough equip load for medium armor.
Your main damage stat for the Fang.
The soft cap. Endgame bosses hit like trucks; this is your insurance.
Extra scaling damage once the essentials are done.

blocks.html: Ignore Mind, Intelligence, Faith, and Arcane entirely. If you ever want to experiment, Rennala (Phase IV) lets you fully respec.

items.html: Two-hand the Fang (hold the interact button while switching weapons). You get 50% bonus Strength scaling and better stagger.
Jumping heavy attacks are your bread and butter — they break enemy poise fast, opening huge critical hits.
Summon before every boss. There is no penalty. Spirit ashes are a core mechanic, not a crutch.
If a fight feels impossible, leave. This guide's route means you're never forced into anything — go level, upgrade, come back.

blocks.label: The Golden Rule
paragraphs: Elden Ring's real difficulty setting is preparation. Flask upgrades (Golden Seeds & Sacred Tears), weapon upgrades (Somber Stones), and Vigor matter far more than skill. This guide front-loads all three.

blocks.html: The opening hours set up your entire run: your mount, your flask, your spirit summons, and — most importantly — your endgame weapon.

items.html: Tutorial: After the Chapel of Anticipation, drop into the glowing pit in the Stranded Graveyard for the Cave of Knowledge tutorial. Do it — it teaches guard counters and stealth, and the Soldier of Godrick at the end is free runes.
First Steps grace → Church of Elleh: Buy the Crafting Kit (300 runes) from Kalé the merchant. Craft supplies will save your life all game.

Gatefront Ruins: Grab the West Limgrave Map Fragment at the roadside stele, and the Whetstone Knife from the cellar under the ruins (lets you swap Ashes of War). Rest at the Gatefront grace — Melina appears, granting leveling and Torrent, your horse.

Return to Church of Elleh at night: The witch Renna sits on the wall. She gives you the Spirit Calling Bell and Lone Wolf Ashes — your boss-fight companions for the next twenty hours. (If she doesn't appear, rest at Gatefront until nightfall and ride back.)

Third Church of Marika (far northeast Limgrave, follow the road east then north): Collect the Flask of Wondrous Physick, the Crimson Crystal Tear (mix it in — a free half-heal once per rest), and a Sacred Tear from the statue (upgrade flask healing at any grace).

Golden Seeds: Small glowing golden saplings mark them. Grab the one on the path up Stormhill toward the castle, and another by Fort Haight in the southeast. Every seed = an extra flask charge. Make a habit: see a golden sapling, take the seed.

Fort Haight (southeast, in Mistwood): Fight or sneak to the tower top for the Dectus Medallion (Left). This is half your ticket to the Altus Plateau later. Two birds, one trip: the Golden Seed sapling is just outside.
Murkwater Cave (in the river ravine north of Agheel Lake): The "boss" is Patches. Beat him down until he surrenders, then spare him. He becomes a merchant — you'll be back for something special.
Stormhill Shack (on the road to Stormveil): Talk to Roderika and exhaust her dialogue. She later moves to the Roundtable Hold and unlocks spirit ash upgrades — essential for the Mimic Tear.

blocks.text: The main event: claim Bloodhound's Fang

blocks.subtitle: Evergaol Duel · Recommended Level 12–18
blocks.name: Bloodhound Knight Darriwil
paragraphs: The Forlorn Hound Evergaol sits on the southern cliffs of Limgrave, just northeast of the Bridge of Sacrifice. Activate the seal to enter. No spirit summons work inside — this one's on you.
tips: He's fast but fragile. Stay locked on, hold your shield up, and punish the end of his lunging combos with one or two hits — then back off.
His long leaping slash is your biggest opening: dodge toward him and counter.
If you struggle, level Vigor a few more times and return — the arena isn't going anywhere.
blocks.footer: Reward: Bloodhound's Fang. Equip it the moment you meet Str 18 / Dex 17. This weapon carries you to the credits.

blocks.html: The Forlorn Hound Evergaol sits on the southern cliffs of Limgrave, just northeast of the Bridge of Sacrifice. Activate the seal to enter. No spirit summons work inside — this one's on you.
Reward: Bloodhound's Fang. Equip it the moment you meet Str 18 / Dex 17. This weapon carries you to the credits.

blocks.label: Efficiency Tip · Margit's Shackle
paragraphs: Once you have ~5,000 runes, return to Patches in Murkwater Cave and buy Margit's Shackle. Used in the first phase of the Margit fight, it pins him to the ground for a free combo — and it works twice. Even better: it works again on a certain boss in Leyndell, much later. Best 5,000 runes you'll ever spend.

items.html: Vigor at 20, and Str 18 / Dex 17 reached — Fang in hand, two-handed.
Flask split: with 4+ charges, keep them all Crimson (healing). You have no use for FP flasks yet beyond one charge for summons — one Cerulean is plenty.

Fang upgraded to +1 or +2 with any Somber Smithing Stones [1]/[2] you've found (Smithing Master Hewg at the Roundtable Hold does upgrades — you're taken there automatically after resting at graces enough / meeting Melina).

blocks.html: Entirely optional territory — which is exactly why it's the perfect low-risk farm for flask upgrades and levels before Stormveil. Ride the loop, grab everything, leave.

items.html: Cross the Bridge of Sacrifice south of Limgrave. Grab the map fragment from the stele on the main road south.
Church of Pilgrimage (northwest corner of the peninsula): a Sacred Tear at the statue.
Fourth Church of Marika (western coast): another Sacred Tear.
Golden Seeds: two saplings sit along the peninsula's roads — one near the Minor Erdtree at the center, one on the route toward Castle Morne. Watch for the glow.
Minor Erdtree (optional): the Erdtree Avatar guarding it drops crystal tears for your Physick. Fight it with Torrent — hit and run. Skip if it's going badly; nothing here is required.
Castle Morne: skip it. Its reward is a Strength weapon you don't need. (If you enjoy side content, the Irina & Edgar questline starts on the road south — but it's not on our critical path.)

blocks.label: Checkpoint
paragraphs: Leaving the peninsula you should be around level 20–25, with 5–6 flask charges healing at +2 or +3 potency. That turns Margit from a wall into a speed bump.

blocks.html: Your first true test — and your first Great Rune. With the Fang, the wolves, and the Shackle, both bosses fold.

blocks.subtitle: Castle Gatekeeper · Recommended Level 20–25
blocks.name: Margit, the Fell Omen
tips: Before the fog: summon the Lone Wolf Ashes, and use Sorcerer Rogier's gold summon sign near the stairs.
Open with Margit's Shackle — he's pinned, free jumping heavies. Use the second charge when he gets back up. That's often a third of his health gone before he swings.
He delays his attacks to bait early dodges. Watch the weapon, not his body — dodge when it moves, not when it glows.
In phase two, his hammer slam has a long recovery. That's your combo window.

items.html: At the main gate, Gatekeeper Gostoc opens a side path — take it. The main gate is a ballista killbox.
Work upward through the rafters and courtyards, resting at each grace. A Golden Seed sits at a sapling in the castle grounds near the Rampart Tower area — keep your eyes open for the glow.

Optional: the Iron Whetblade in the armory (behind a locked door — the key is in the room where the hawk-knights nest) lets you put Heavy/Keen affinities on regular weapons. Not needed for the Fang, but grab it if you pass by; future builds thank you.
Talk to Nepheli Loux in the room before the boss — this puts her summon sign outside the fog.

blocks.subtitle: Shardbearer · Recommended Level 25–30
blocks.name: Godrick the Grafted
tips: Summon Nepheli (sign by the fog gate) plus your wolves. Three targets, one very confused demigod.
Stay behind him and to his left; nearly everything he does swings forward and right.
Phase two he grafts a dragon head onto his arm — when he winds up the flame sweep, sprint behind him and attack freely. The fire breath is a damage window for you, not him.
blocks.footer: After the fight: take Godrick's Great Rune, then ride east from the castle across the long bridge to the Divine Tower of Limgrave to activate it. Equipped with a Rune Arc, it raises all your stats. Also visit Nepheli at the Roundtable Hold afterward — she gives you the Arsenal Charm (extra equip load).

blocks.html: After the fight: take Godrick's Great Rune, then ride east from the castle across the long bridge to the Divine Tower of Limgrave to activate it. Equipped with a Rune Arc, it raises all your stats. Also visit Nepheli at the Roundtable Hold afterward — she gives you the Arsenal Charm (extra equip load).

phases.title: Phase IV — Liurnia of the Lakes

blocks.html: A huge region — but our business here is short: seeds, tears, a key, and the easiest demigod in the game, who happens to hand you unlimited free respecs.

items.html: Exit Stormveil north into Liurnia. Grab map fragments from road steles (one on the main road north, more in the west and east).
Golden Seeds line the main northern road — Liurnia has several saplings. Same habit: glow means seed.
Check every ruined Church of Marika (small chapels with the goddess statue) for Sacred Tears as you ride — there's one in the region's north.

The Academy Glintstone Key: on a small island west of the academy, a colossal dragon sleeps on a pile of treasure. Ride in quietly, grab the key beside it, ride out. Do not wake the dragon. (Or do — I'm a guide, not a cop. But don't.)
Fight through the academy — sorcerers die in one or two Fang combos. Mid-dungeon boss Red Wolf of Radagon: summon wolves, stay aggressive, dodge through its blade attacks. It has low health.

blocks.subtitle: Shardbearer · Recommended Level 35–40
blocks.name: Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon
tips: Phase one isn't a fight, it's a puzzle: smash the chanting students with golden glows to break her shield, then wail on her. Repeat.
Phase two allows spirit summons — bring the wolves. Rush her down between spells; she has very little health and no poise.
blocks.footer: Reward: her Great Rune (doesn't count for Leyndell entry, but that's fine — Radahn's will) and, crucially, Rebirth: unlimited stat respecs using Larval Tears. If you ever misallocate levels, Rennala fixes it.

blocks.html: Reward: her Great Rune (doesn't count for Leyndell entry, but that's fine — Radahn's will) and, crucially, Rebirth: unlimited stat respecs using Larval Tears. If you ever misallocate levels, Rennala fixes it.

blocks.label: Optional Detour · Ranni
paragraphs: Northwest of Liurnia, at Ranni's Rise, you can begin Ranni's questline — the game's best side story with a legendary ending. It's not on the critical path, so this guide skips it, but if you want one questline, it's this one.

blocks.html: Caelid is a rotting hellscape, and we're going in anyway — because killing Radahn unlocks the city where the Mimic Tear sleeps, and Caelid's side loot is absurdly good.

items.html: Ride to Fort Faroth in Dragonbarrow (northeast Caelid — approach via the ravine road from Limgrave's north, or the beast-statue teleporter behind the Third Church). Climb to the roof, drop through the hole, and loot the Dectus Medallion (Right) and Radagon's Soreseal — a talisman granting +5 to Vigor, Endurance, Strength, and Dexterity for 15% extra damage taken. On this build, wear it for most of the game.

Rune windfall: outside Fort Faroth lies Greyoll, a colossal sleeping dragon. Carve at her tail (she never fights back — her hatchlings do, so stay at the tail tip) for a massive rune payout plus a Dragon Heart. This alone funds several levels.

With both medallion halves, warp to the Grand Lift of Dectus (far north Liurnia), ride it up, and touch the first Altus Plateau grace. Then warp straight back out. This guarantees the Radahn Festival is live at Redmane Castle.

Sellia Crystal Tunnel (eastern Caelid): fight to the end and beat the Fallingstar Beast (bring the wolves; hug its flank). It drops the Somberstone Miner's Bell Bearing [1] — hand it to the Twin Maiden Husks at the Roundtable to buy low-tier Somber stones forever. Your Fang upgrade path just became a shopping list.
Ride south to Redmane Castle — with the festival live, the front is peaceful. Talk to the announcer, then take the teleporter to the beach.

blocks.subtitle: Shardbearer · Recommended Level 50–60 · Fang +5 or higher
blocks.name: Starscourge Radahn
tips: This fight is designed around the gold NPC summon signs scattered across the battlefield. Summon every single one, and re-summon them when they fall — the signs reappear as the fight goes on.
Stay on Torrent. Let the summons hold his attention while you ride in, land a jumping heavy, and ride out.
At half health he leaves the arena and crashes back down as a meteor. Ride away from the targeting shadow, then resume the routine.
You are the distraction manager, not the hero. Patience wins this in two or three attempts.
blocks.footer: Reward: Radahn's Great Rune — your second qualifying rune for Leyndell — and a cutscene: a falling star punches a hole in Limgrave. That crater is our next stop.

blocks.html: Reward: Radahn's Great Rune — your second qualifying rune for Leyndell — and a cutscene: a falling star punches a hole in Limgrave. That crater is our next stop.
One dungeon, one objective: the single best item in Elden Ring. Everything after this fight alongside a copy of yourself.

items.html: Travel to the crater southeast of Fort Haight in Mistwood, Limgrave. Descend the floating ruins carefully — fall damage is the real boss here.
Fight through Nokron's outskirts. The Mimic Tear boss guarding the bridge copies your gear — a cheeky trick: unequip your weapon before the fog, let it spawn unarmed, then re-equip and destroy it.
Continue across the Ancestral Woods, then follow the cliffside path down toward Night's Sacred Ground.
In the ruined chamber there, a chest behind an imp-sealed door (1 Stonesword Key — buy from merchants or Twin Maiden Husks if you're out) holds the Mimic Tear Ashes. Mission complete.

While you're down here, harvest every Ghost Glovewort flower you see in graveyard areas — these upgrade the Mimic via Roderika's spirit tuning at the Roundtable. Get it to +3 or higher now, and keep feeding it gloveworts all game. A +10 Mimic is functionally a second player.

blocks.label: How the Mimic Works
paragraphs: It clones whatever you're wearing and holding when summoned. It has no FP cost — it costs a chunk of HP instead, which your flask refills. Summon it at every boss fog for the rest of the run. From here on, when a boss card says "summon," it means the Mimic.

blocks.html: Back up the Grand Lift of Dectus, this time to stay. Altus is dense with flask upgrades — the richest seed-and-tear territory in the game.

items.html: Grab the Altus map fragment from the stele on the main road heading northeast from the lift.

Golden Seeds everywhere: Altus and the capital outskirts hold more saplings than any other region — several along the main roads alone, including a multi-seed sapling. Your flask should reach 10+ charges before Leyndell.
Check the region's ruined churches — the Second Church of Marika in the west holds a Sacred Tear, and more chapels dot the plateau. Target: +8 flask potency or better before the capital.
Altus Tunnel (north-central): more Somber stones and a mid-tier Somberstone bell bearing region — mine what you pass; the Fang wants to be +7 by the end of this phase.

Skip for efficiency: the Shaded Castle, Mt. Gelmir, and Volcano Manor (Rykard) are all optional. If you're under-leveled later, Gelmir is your side-quest bank — but our two Great Runes are already banked.
Follow the main road east until the capital's outer wall. Rest at the outskirts graces.

blocks.html: Two Great Runes open the gates. Inside: golden streets, a dragon on the wall, and a familiar face waiting at the throne.

blocks.subtitle: Gatekeeper · Fight Mounted
blocks.name: Draconic Tree Sentinel
tips: Guards the capital's main gate. Stay on Torrent the entire fight — circle him, dart in for single jumping attacks, dart out.
Respect the lightning in phase two; keep moving laterally and never linger behind him (the horse kicks).

items.html: Climb the great dragon wing to reach the upper city. Rest at every grace — Leyndell's shortcuts are a maze and graces are your breadcrumbs.
Golden Seeds: the capital holds two more saplings within its streets. The glow habit continues indoors.
Godfrey, First Elden Lord (golden shade): a warm-up boss. Mimic + jumping heavies. He staggers constantly.

blocks.subtitle: Shardbearer · Recommended Level 80+ · Fang +7
blocks.name: Morgott, the Omen King
tips: Remember Margit? Same Omen, bigger crown — and Margit's Shackle still works. Two free pin-downs in phase one.
Summon Melina's gold sign outside the fog, plus your Mimic. Three-on-one.
He's aggressive but light — jumping heavies stagger him into criticals repeatedly. Dodge his cursed-sword rain by sprinting sideways.
blocks.footer: After the fight: speak to Melina at the grace — she grants the Rold Medallion, your key to the Mountaintops. Before you leave: this is a great moment to bank flask potency, ghost gloveworts, and any capital loot, because the city changes forever near the end of the game.

blocks.html: After the fight: speak to Melina at the grace — she grants the Rold Medallion, your key to the Mountaintops. Before you leave: this is a great moment to bank flask potency, ghost gloveworts, and any capital loot, because the city changes forever near the end of the game.

phases.title: Phase IX — Mountaintops of the Giants

blocks.html: The Grand Lift of Rold carries you into the endgame. The Mountaintops are linear — follow the road, loot the chapels, and prepare to fight a mountain.

items.html: Take the Grand Lift of Rold (east of the capital, hoist the Rold Medallion). Grab the map fragment past Zamor Ruins.

Follow the main path across the great chain-bridge and around the valley. More Golden Seeds and a chapel Sacred Tear await along the route — by now your flask should be nearly maxed (target: 12+ charges, +10 or higher potency).
Level check: Vigor 50–60 before the Fire Giant. His attacks are the hardest-hitting yet. Greyoll's runes or a lap of skipped Altus content will top you up fast.

blocks.subtitle: Guardian of the Forge · Recommended Level 95–105 · Fang +8/+9
blocks.name: Fire Giant
tips: Phase one: stay mounted, and attack the glowing shackle on his left ankle — it takes bonus damage. Ride away when he rolls (yes, he rolls; yes, it's terrifying).

Phase two (he tears himself apart): dismount, summon the Mimic, and live under his hands and feet. Almost everything dangerous happens far away from his body — the safest place is uncomfortably close.
The fireball barrage tracks — sprint perpendicular, don't roll early.
blocks.footer: After the fight: rest at the Forge of the Giants grace and speak with Melina. Warning: committing here transports you to Crumbling Farum Azula and permanently transforms Leyndell — several capital-based NPC quests close forever. If any side business matters to you, finish it first. When ready, commit.

blocks.html: After the fight: rest at the Forge of the Giants grace and speak with Melina. Warning: committing here transports you to Crumbling Farum Azula and permanently transforms Leyndell — several capital-based NPC quests close forever. If any side business matters to you, finish it first. When ready, commit.
A city shattered across the sky, patrolled by dragons and beastmen. The final Somber Ancient Dragon stone for your +10 Fang is found here — keep your eyes on side rooms and corpses as you climb.

items.html: Progress grace to grace — the level loops back on itself with elevators. Beastmen hit hard in groups; pull them one at a time.
Loot thoroughly for Somber Smithing Stones [8]/[9] and a Somber Ancient Dragon Smithing Stone — your ticket to Bloodhound's Fang +10.
Optional: Dragonlord Placidusax hides below the floating debris near the dragon temple — a spectacular but skippable fight. Not on our path.

blocks.subtitle: Duo Gauntlet · Sleep Is Legal
blocks.name: Godskin Duo
tips: The most-hated fight in the game — unless you know the secret: both Godskins can be put to sleep. Craft Sleep Pots (Trina's Lilies + the pot recipe from Fevor's Cookbook) or just brute-force it.
Brute-force plan: summon the Mimic, and both of you focus the Noble (the fat one) first. Kill him fast, then two-on-one the Apostle.
Use the pillars — the Noble's rolling attack shatters on them.

blocks.subtitle: Death Incarnate · Recommended Level 110+
blocks.name: Maliketh, the Black Blade
tips: Phase one (Beast Clergyman) is simple: stay close, punish his dagger combos, break his poise with jumping heavies.
Phase two, Maliketh goes airborne and hits like doom itself. Summon the Mimic before the fog, let it take aggro, and attack only after his aerial strings end — he's very vulnerable on landing.
His Destined Death attacks lower your max HP on hit — respect them, chip patiently, and this ends fast: he has one of the smallest health pools of any endgame boss.
blocks.footer: After the fight: a portal at the site of grace returns you to the capital of ash. The end of the road is in sight.

blocks.html: After the fight: a portal at the site of grace returns you to the capital of ash. The end of the road is in sight.
Ashen Leyndell. Four fights stand between you and the throne. Final loadout check: Fang +10, Mimic +10, flask maxed, Vigor 60, Radagon's Soreseal traded out if you're feeling fragile.

items.html: Sir Gideon Ofnir, the All-Knowing: a mage in a boss room. Sprint at him, stunlock, done. The shortest "boss" in the game.

blocks.subtitle: First Elden Lord · For Real This Time
blocks.name: Godfrey / Hoarah Loux
tips: Phase one Godfrey is honest — dodge the axe, punish the stomps' recovery.
Phase two he throws the axe away and wrestles you. His grabs cannot be blocked — dodge them late, the wind-ups are long and theatrical.
The Mimic shines here: while he suplexes your clone, you get free combos. Poor clone.

blocks.subtitle: The Final Battle · Two Bosses, One Fog Gate
blocks.name: Radagon of the Golden Order → The Elden Beast
tips: Radagon staggers beautifully to jumping heavy attacks — he's a pure physical brawl. Dodge into his hammer slams. His teleport-grab telegraphs with a golden ring; sprint away from it.
Save your Mimic's health where you can — it carries over into part two. (If it dies, it dies; you can't re-summon between phases.)
The Elden Beast swims away constantly — you can summon Torrent in this arena to close distance instead of jogging across the ocean of stars.
When it casts Elden Stars (a comet stream from above), sprint directly toward and past the beast — the stars chase your old position.
Attack the glowing brand on its body when it surfaces. Patience, chip damage, and hydration.
blocks.footer: Then touch the fractured Marika, and choose your ending. Mend the ring however you see fit, Elden Lord.

blocks.html: Then touch the fractured Marika, and choose your ending. Mend the ring however you see fit, Elden Lord.

rows: Imp-sealed room, Summonwater Village catacombs (Limgrave, 1 key) — faster stamina regen, useful forever
Nepheli at the Roundtable — carry heavier armor without fat-rolling
Fort Faroth — +5 to your four core stats; the 15% damage-taken tax is worth it until the final bosses
Any HP / defense talisman you find
Crimson Amber Medallions and defensive charms round out the fourth slot as slots unlock
Third Church of Marika — your default all game
Minor Erdtree bosses drop tears as you travel — pick whichever utility tear you've collected; a stamina-regen or damage-reduction tear pairs well
One massive payout — carve the sleeping dragon's tail tip
Weeping Peninsula, Mt. Gelmir, and Caelid side dungeons are level banks you deliberately saved
Not farming, but +all stats makes every fight faster

blocks.text: E. The five commandments of not dying

items.html: Vigor before damage. A dead Tarnished deals zero DPS.
See a golden sapling → take the seed. See a ruined church → check the statue.
Summon at every fog gate. The game balances around it.
Stuck for 5+ attempts? Leave. Level, upgrade, return. The wall crumbles on its own.
Dodge on the enemy's swing, not their wind-up. Every boss in this game delays on purpose.
